project_subtitle: A calm companion for returning to missed prayers and fasts, with guided onboarding, privacy-aware tracking, and steady progress you can sustain.
why_body: Awdah was built to make qadaa feel less overwhelming and more manageable, one honest step at a time. The goal is a compassionate, private experience that respects the weight of this journey while giving you the structure to keep moving without shame, noise, or pressure.
who_body: Whether you are returning after a long gap, trying to estimate your starting point with care, or simply looking for a clearer way to stay consistent, Awdah helps you begin with clarity and continue with steadiness.

features.title: Salah Tracking
features.body: Track missed obligatory prayers with a clear view of what has been completed, what remains, and how your effort is building over time.

features.title: Sawm Tracking
features.body: Log missed Ramadan fasts with dedicated progress tracking and a focused picture of what still needs to be made up.

features.title: Hijri First
features.body: Built around the Islamic calendar, with accurate dual-date support to help qadaa records stay grounded in the dates that matter.

features.title: Dynamic Language Support
features.body: Designed for a multilingual product experience, with Arabic, English, and German supported today, and the language system ready to grow cleanly as new communities contribute.

features.title: Privacy First
features.body: Awdah is designed to stay respectful of sensitive religious data, with minimal collection, clear account controls, and privacy-aware flows from onboarding onward.

features.title: Onboarding & Guidance
features.body: A guided onboarding flow helps users set a starting point thoughtfully, while supporting content explains the terms, assumptions, and rulings behind the tracking experience.

features.title: Fully Self-Hostable
features.body: Run the entire stack locally with a single docker compose up command. LocalStack simulates every AWS service the app uses, so you get full functionality: DynamoDB, auth, backups, without an AWS account, without sending data anywhere, and with the same performance as the hosted version.

team.name: Amgad Mahmoud
team.bio:
Awdah came from a personal place: a need for something structured and calm that could support a spiritual journey without adding pressure. So I designed and built it, not as a prototype, but as a real product meant to last.

Through Awdah, I led end-to-end product engineering: domain modeling for Salah and Sawm workflows, Hijri-first calculation logic, multilingual UX architecture, and privacy-aware user journeys. The platform runs on a serverless AWS stack, Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, Cognito, with CDK-managed infrastructure, CI/CD automation, observability, and deliberate cost design.

Outside of Awdah, my work sits at the intersection of cloud-native architecture and digital transformation: multi-account platform integration for enterprises, infrastructure-as-code with AWS CDK and CDKTF, and security-gated delivery pipelines. I care about clean architecture, backend systems that scale, and turning meaningful ideas into products that people can actually rely on.

contribute_body: Awdah is open source. If you would like to help improve it, report a bug, or suggest a feature, check out how to contribute.
